title: Pilosella glaucochroa
intro: Pilosella glaucochroa, also known as Alpine Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential remedy for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella glaucochroa include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pilosella glaucochroa is typically used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Pilosella glaucochroa is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant is also known as a member of the genus Pilosella, which is a group of plants closely related to dandelions. Pilosella glaucochroa is further classified as a species within the Pilosella genus. The Pilosella genus belongs to the subtribe Cichorioideae, which is a large group of flowering plants. Pilosella glaucochroa is native to Europe, where it can be found in various habitats.
